terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.chap: Field Assistant guidance for vehicle prep, route starts, roadside signals, and blocked requirements.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.prep: Get the first route-capable vehicle into the field.

Craft a Vehicle Workbench, then use station processing to turn a Vehicle Frame, two Scrap Tires, and a Fuel Canister into a Scrap Bike Kit. Deploy the kit on flat ground and keep the Terminal close for telemetry.
Vehicle telemetry is online. Scan routes and prep the first blocker.
Deploy the Scrap Bike Kit on flat ground, then stand near it with the terminal.
Use the Vehicle Workbench to process a frame, two Scrap Tires, and a Fuel Canister into a Scrap Bike Kit.
Craft a Vehicle Workbench from iron, copper, redstone, and a piston.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.rout: Load the vehicle, resolve route blockers, and launch a contract.
The route board explains the first blocker it finds. Fuel comes from Fuel Canisters. Cargo can be loaded by sneak-using an item on your owned vehicle or through a Cargo Anchor.
A route is active. Drive to the next signal marker before starting another.
Ready: start %s from the Convoy tab or a nearby Convoy Beacon.
No unclaimed route definitions are loaded.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.clos: Close active route legs at Roadside Signal Markers, then claim the once-only payout.
Drive at least the route leg's required distance from the start point. The paired vehicle must be nearby, and checkpoint routes may need faction reputation before the final signal closes.
Claim the %s payout. Rewards cannot be claimed twice.
Start a convoy route before looking for signal closeout.
Drive to %s, then log the matching Roadside Signal Marker from the Convoy tab.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.rewa: Convoy tabs and route records can now guide the next step.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.mess: ECHO CONVOY // No Roadside Signal Marker nearby. Drive to the route marker, then open the terminal again.
ECHO CONVOY // No convoy rewards are ready to claim.
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.requ: Deploy a vehicle kit and stand within telemetry range
Reload data packs if this remains empty
Add Fuel Canisters directly or through a Vehicle Dock
Start a route before using SIGNAL
Move terminal range to the active vehicle or relink with Route Beacon
Use the route-matching marker after the distance requirement
Claim after the final signal closes
terminal.echoconvoyprotocol.mission.read: Ready: start this route at a Convoy Beacon or from the Convoy tab.
Craft a Vehicle Workbench, process a Scrap Bike Kit, deploy it, then stand near the vehicle.
Bring a %s kit or choose a route for the current vehicle.
Add Fuel Canisters directly or through a Vehicle Dock until fuel reaches %s.
Load %s by sneak-using cargo on the vehicle or with a Cargo Anchor.
Build %s reputation or select an unguarded route first.
Review route requirements in the Convoy tab, then scan again.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.vehicle_workb: Processes parts into deployable convoy vehicle kits.
First craft this block, then process a frame, tires, and fuel into a Scrap Bike Kit.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.fuel_still: Turns burnable stock into Fuel Canisters for route starts.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.fuel_still.hi: Use when the route board says fuel is short; load canisters directly or through a Vehicle Dock.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.battery_charg: Processes redstone into Battery Cells for rover power and relay tools.
Carry cells for battery vehicles and relay deployments before long road work.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.vehicle_dock: Transfers fuel, charge, and repairs into nearby owned vehicles.
Place beside your vehicle, add canisters, cells, or repair kits, then scan routes again.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.convoy_beacon: Starts the first ready route for a nearby owned vehicle.
If nothing starts, open Convoy Routes; Field Assistant lists the missing fuel, cargo, or vehicle.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.roadside_sign: Closes the next active route leg for the paired vehicle.
Use SIGNAL after driving far enough from the start and matching the active route leg.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.cargo_anchor: Loads or unloads cargo for nearby owned vehicles without deleting overflow.
Use when Field Assistant reports missing cargo; add cargo items or a Cargo Net to change direction.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.field_repair_: Consumes Convoy Repair Kits to repair nearby owned vehicles.
Useful before high-threat routes; relay trucks can deploy a charged station in the field.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.scrap_tire: Starter wheel component for Scrap Bike and rover workbench processing.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.scrap_tire.hi: Make two for the first Scrap Bike Kit.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.armored_tire: Reinforced wheel component for heavier convoy vehicles and route rewards.
Keep these for rover, crawler, and relay truck progression.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.vehicle_frame: Base chassis component for every Vehicle Workbench vehicle kit recipe.
Pair with tires and route fuel to create your first deployable vehicle.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.fuel_canister: Restores vehicle fuel directly or through a Vehicle Dock.
Route starts validate fuel server-side; scan again after fueling.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.battery_cell: Restores vehicle battery charge directly or through a Vehicle Dock.
Battery vehicles and relay actions may fail until charge is restored.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.engine_core: Mid-tier vehicle component and Salvager Escort cargo.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.engine_core.h: Load one into a Wasteland Rover when Field Assistant flags Salvager Escort cargo.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.radiation_shi: Installs on supported vehicles to reduce road hazard damage.
Use directly on your owned vehicle before dangerous or checkpoint routes.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.convoy_repair: Repairs vehicle damage directly, at a dock, or from a Field Repair Station.
Carry a few before starting routes with road ambush threats.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.cargo_net: Switches Cargo Anchors toward vehicle unload and Logistics handoff behavior.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.cargo_net.hin: Use when cargo should leave the vehicle instead of being loaded into it.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.route_beacon: Scans compatible routes or pairs an active route to the vehicle you use it on.
Use this if terminal telemetry says the active route vehicle is missing.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.scrap_bike_ki: Deploys the starter vehicle for early Convoy routes.
Craft through Vehicle Workbench processing, then use on flat ground.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.wasteland_rov: Deploys a balanced vehicle with cargo, shielding, and scanner range.
Use for routes that require rover cargo or longer scanner reach.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.cargo_crawler: Deploys a slow heavy vehicle for freight routes.
Prep this when route board cargo requirements outgrow starter vehicles.
tooltip.echoconvoyprotocol.armored_relay: Deploys a durable relay vehicle with field station support.
Charge and stock it before high-risk support runs.
